summary: Additive pipeline abstraction on top of existing analyser module. No structural changes to the audio graph, no new files, no breaking changes to existing exports. The plan correctly identifies the per-frame allocation issue in getFrequencyData/getTimeDomainData and solves it with a pre-allocated buffer wrapper.

modified_contracts: AudioPlayer interface gains getPipeline() — additive, non-breaking
architectural_impact.dependency_changes: None — uses only existing Web Audio API types

strengths: Pre-allocated buffers eliminate GC pressure in the render loop, aligning with CLAUDE.md performance guidance
Extends existing files rather than creating new ones — no module proliferation
Keeps existing getFrequencyData/getTimeDomainData exports for backward compatibility
poll() design decouples audio sampling from rendering — clean separation of concerns
Test-first approach with reuse of existing mock helpers
All 4 acceptance criteria are covered by multiple tests across both test files

suggestions.description: The existing getFrequencyData/getTimeDomainData functions will become effectively dead code once the pipeline is the primary consumer. Consider marking them with a deprecation comment or removing them in a follow-up story if no other callers exist.

Step 3 adds getPipeline() to the player but the renderLoop.ts integration is not in scope for this story. Ensure a follow-up story (likely the visual mapping story) wires pipeline.poll() into the frame callback.

files_impact.src/audio/analyser.ts: Add AnalyserPipeline interface and createPipeline() factory — additive only
files_impact.src/audio/player.ts: Add getPipeline() to AudioPlayer interface and wire in initAudio() — minor addition
files_impact.tests/audio/analyser.test.ts: Add new describe block for pipeline tests — extends existing file
files_impact.tests/audio/player.test.ts: Add pipeline-related tests — extends existing file
